guidePratique.title: Practical Guide for Gabonese in France
guidePratique.description: Essential information to facilitate your settlement and daily life in France. The consulate supports you in all your procedures.
guidePratique.cta: Need help with a specific procedure? Our consular team is at your service.

logement.title: Housing
logement.desc: Finding accommodation, housing aid (APL/ALS), tenant rights and administrative procedures.

sante.title: Health & Social Protection
sante.desc: Social security registration, health insurance, access to care and health coverage rights.

education.title: Education & Training
education.desc: School enrollment, scholarships, recognition of Gabonese diplomas and vocational training.

emploi.title: Employment & Entrepreneurship
emploi.desc: Job search, business creation, work permits and employment assistance.

droits.title: Rights & Residency Permit
droits.desc: Residence permit renewal, family reunification, naturalization and free legal aid.

famille.title: Family & Children
famille.desc: Birth registration, family benefits, childcare and transcription of civil status documents.

faq.title: Most asked questions
faq.description: Quickly find answers to your questions about consular procedures.
faq.contactDescription: Contact the General Consulate of Gabon for any additional information request.

inscription.question: How do I register at the consular register?
inscription.answer: Registration can be done online at consulat.ga or in person at the consulate. Bring your valid passport, proof of address in France less than 3 months old, and 2 ID photos. The procedure is free and gives you access to consular protection.

passeport.question: What documents are needed to renew my passport?
passeport.answer: For passport renewal, you must provide: the old passport (even expired), a copy of your birth certificate, 2 compliant ID photos, a recent proof of address, and the completed application form. An appointment at the DGDI office is required for fingerprinting.

etatCivil.question: How to transcribe a French birth certificate in Gabon?
etatCivil.answer: Transcription of a birth certificate allows it to be recognized in Gabon. Submit to the consulate: the full copy of the French birth certificate, parents' identity documents, and family record book if applicable. The average processing time is 2 to 4 weeks.

urgence.question: What to do in case of a consular emergency?
urgence.answer: In case of emergency (serious accident, death, arrest, natural disaster), immediately contact the consular emergency number at 07 44 23 95 84, available 24/7. The consulate can assist you, repatriate remains, or facilitate contact with French authorities.

rdv.question: Do I need an appointment to visit the consulate?
rdv.answer: An appointment is strongly recommended for passport and visa procedures. For other services (legalization, civil status, consular registration), you can come during opening hours Monday to Friday from 9am to 4pm. Book online at consulat.ga for faster service.

vote.question: How to vote from France?
vote.answer: To participate in Gabonese elections from France, you must be registered at the consular register and on the consular electoral roll. During elections, the consulate sets up polling stations. Present yourself with your consular card and valid ID on election day.

regularisation.question: How to regularize my status in France?
regularisation.answer: Several paths exist: exceptional admission (Valls circular 2012), regularization through employment, family reasons, medical grounds. File at the prefecture or online via the ANEF portal. Consult a specialized lawyer.

frontiere.question: What documents to present at the border?
frontiere.answer: Valid passport, visa, accommodation certificate, return ticket (short stay), travel insurance (min. €30,000). Resources: min. €120/day (hotel) or €32.50/day (private accommodation with attestation).

carteSejour.question: How to renew my residence permit?
carteSejour.answer: Renewal is done 2 to 4 months before expiry, online at the ANEF portal or at the prefecture. A receipt is issued while waiting for the new permit.

oqtf.question: What is an OQTF and what to do if I receive one?
oqtf.answer: The OQTF is an administrative order to leave France. NEVER ignore it. Appeal: to the prefect (30 days) or administrative court. The appeal is suspensive. Contact a lawyer and the consulate immediately.

perteDocuments.question: I lost my passport/residence permit, what do I do?
perteDocuments.answer: Report to the police station, then contact the consulate (passport) or prefecture/ANEF (residence permit). Always keep digital copies of your documents.

travailEtudiant.question: I'm a student, how many hours can I work?
travailEtudiant.answer: 964 hours per year (about 20h/week, 60% of legal working time). No additional work permit needed — your student permit authorizes it.

visaBinational.question: I'm a dual national, do I need a visa for Gabon?
visaBinational.answer: No, with a valid Gabonese passport. With only a French passport, an e-Visa is required (evisa.dgdi.ga). The consulate can help with obtaining a Gabonese passport.

arrestation.question: What to do in case of arrest?
arrestation.answer: Stay calm. Rights: know the reason, remain silent, have a lawyer, notify a relative, contact the consulate (Vienna Convention, art. 36). Police custody: 24h max (renewable once). Don't sign anything without understanding.

changementStatut.question: How to switch from student to employee status?
changementStatut.answer: Job related to your degree, salary ≥ 1.5x minimum wage (1x for shortage occupations). Employer requests work authorization, then status change at prefecture. Timeline: 2 to 4 months.

aps.question: What is the APS and how to benefit from it?
aps.answer: The APS is a 12-month permit (non-renewable) for Master's degree holders. Allows job search or business creation related to degree. Apply before student permit expires.

integration.heroDescription: Complete guide for Gabonese nationals living in France: your rights, procedures, cultural codes and practical tips for successful integration. The General Consulate of Gabon supports you.

accessibilite.title: Accessibility
accessibilite.intro: The General Consulate of Gabon in France is committed to making this website accessible in accordance with current standards.

conformance.title: Compliance Statement
conformance.content: This website has been designed to be accessible to the widest audience. We strive to comply with the RGAA (General Accessibility Improvement Framework) recommendations.

technologies.title: Technologies Used
technologies.content: This site is built with the following modern web technologies:

navigation.title: Navigation
navigation.content: Full keyboard navigation, hierarchical heading structure, skip links.

visual.title: Display
visual.content: AA-compliant contrasts, dark mode, resizable text, responsive design.

assistive.title: Assistive Technologies
assistive.content: Compatible with screen readers (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver), text alternatives for images.

contact.title: Contact
contact.content: If you encounter an accessibility issue, please contact us:

legal.title: Legal Remedies
legal.content: If you notice an accessibility defect preventing you from accessing content or a feature of the site, you may contact the Defender of Rights.

confidentialite.title: Privacy Policy
confidentialite.intro: The General Consulate of Gabon in France is committed to protecting the privacy of users of this website.

dataCollection.title: Data Collection
dataCollection.content: We only collect data necessary for the operation of consular services: surname, first name, date of birth, nationality, contact details, identity documents.

purpose.title: Purpose of Processing
purpose.content: Your data is used exclusively for:

retention.title: Data Retention Period
retention.content: Your data is kept for the duration necessary to process your request, then archived in accordance with applicable legal obligations.

rights.title: Your Rights
rights.content: In accordance with the GDPR, you have the following rights:

cookies.title: Cookies
cookies.content: This site uses technical cookies necessary for its operation and anonymous audience measurement cookies. No advertising cookies are used.

contact.title: DPO Contact
contact.content: To exercise your rights or for any question regarding the protection of your data:

editor.title: Website Publisher
editor.content: General Consulate of the Gabonese Republic in France

director.title: Publication Director
director.content: The Consul General of the Gabonese Republic in France

hosting.title: Hosting
hosting.content: This site is hosted by Firebase (Google Cloud Platform), 8 rue de Londres, 75009 Paris, France.

intellectual.title: Intellectual Property
intellectual.content: All content on this site (text, images, videos, logos) is protected by copyright. Any reproduction, even partial, requires prior authorization.

responsibility.title: Liability
responsibility.content: The General Consulate endeavours to ensure the accuracy of the information published on this site. However, it cannot be held responsible for errors, omissions or results that may be obtained through misuse of this information.

links.title: Hyperlinks
links.content: This site may contain links to external sites. The General Consulate has no control over these sites and declines any responsibility for their content.

leConsulat.title: The Consulate
leConsulat.subtitle: The General Consulate of the Gabonese Republic in France
leConsulat.description: Serving the Gabonese community in France for over 50 years. Discover our missions, our history and our commitment.
leConsulat.heroDescription: In accordance with the Vienna Convention on Consular Relations, the General Consulate of Gabon in France ensures the protection and assistance of Gabonese nationals, issues official documents and fosters the development of relations between the two countries.
leConsulat.consulMessageTitle: A Word from the Consul General
leConsulat.consulMessage: On behalf of the entire consular team, I welcome you to the official website of the General Consulate of Gabon in France. Our mission is to assist you in all your administrative procedures and to strengthen the bonds between our community and our country. We are at your service.
leConsulat.audienceSubject: Request for an audience with H.E. Mr.
leConsulat.consulRole: Consul General of Gabon in France
leConsulat.consulDescription: A career diplomat, the Consul General ensures the protection and assistance of Gabonese nationals in France. He oversees the strengthening of bilateral relations between Gabon and France and the modernisation of consular services.
leConsulat.missionsSubtitle: Consular functions as defined by the Vienna Convention of 1963

0.title: Protection of Nationals
0.description: Protecting the interests of the Gabonese State and its nationals in France, in accordance with the Vienna Convention of 1963 (art. 5). Assisting detainees, accident victims, and providing support in case of death or repatriation.

1.title: International Relations
1.description: Developing trade, economic, cultural and scientific relations between Gabon and France. Promoting bilateral cooperation and economic diplomacy.

2.title: Official Documents
2.description: Issuing passports, visas, travel documents and laissez-passer documents. Legalisation and certification of official administrative documents for nationals and visitors.

3.title: Civil Status
3.description: Civil Registrar functions: registration of births, celebration of marriages, declaration of deaths and transcription of certificates. Civil status services for the Gabonese community.

4.title: Notarial & Administrative Acts
4.description: Notarial functions for authenticated deeds, powers of attorney, certificates of life, legalisation of signatures and documents. Drafting and certification of administrative acts.

5.title: Emergency Assistance
5.description: Relief and assistance to Gabonese nationals in distress: help for detainees, accident or disaster victims, organisation of repatriations and emergency aid.

6.title: Judicial Functions
6.description: Transmission of judicial and extrajudicial documents, execution of letters rogatory under international conventions. Facilitating legal proceedings between France and Gabon.

protection.title: Consular Protection
protection.description: Assistance and protection of Gabonese nationals in France in all situations.

administrative.title: Administrative Services
administrative.description: Issuance of passports, consular cards, civil status certificates and other official documents.

community.title: Community Life
community.description: Support and guidance for the Gabonese diaspora in France.

cultural.title: Cultural Outreach
cultural.description: Promoting Gabonese culture in France through events and partnerships.

economic.title: Economic Cooperation
economic.description: Facilitating economic exchanges between Gabon and France.

legal.title: Legal Assistance
legal.description: Support with legal and notarial procedures.

social.title: Social Action
social.description: Support for nationals in difficulty and referral to relevant services.

cta.title: Got a question? Need help?
cta.description: Our team is at your disposal to answer all your questions and assist you with your consular procedures.
